hard.opp_name: Nippon Life Insurance Company_ Opp - April '26
hard.next_step: Tailored demo focused only on iSource, iSupplier, iContract, and Autonomous Negotiation Agent in a Nippon-like workflow Deep dive on AI especially how negotiation agent works with real scenarios and controls Share use cases relevant to insurance or services firms with similar scale Map current process to future state (light workshop style) Include procurement + IT stakeholder for integration confidence
hard.ais_why: Nippon is exploring how AI, particularly the Autonomous Negotiation Agent (ANA), can enhance cost savings and consistency in supplier negotiations; the buyer is asking for an ANA-specific deep dive with real scenarios and controls. No Avoma calls tied to this opp yet, so confidence stays Medium because the evidence is Salesforce-only.

north_star_verdict.headline: Early Qualified $100k full-suite deal single-threaded through one Decision Maker (Derrick Staat) who has never answered a live call, all outbound dials logged Could Not Connect through 27 May and the tailored ANA demo still unexecuted; no economic buyer, champion, pain, or competitive read on record.
north_star_verdict.math: Created 2026-04-24 (73 days ago), qualified 2026-04-28; still in Qualified with no stage progression. Close 2026-11-24 (141 days out) after Grace Kim pushed it from 2026-09-25 on 2026-05-11. Last activity 2026-05-27 (40 days ago), and every logged buyer touch is an outbound Could Not Connect, so there has been zero two-way buyer dialogue across the deal's life. Not yet 60 days cold, so Slowing rather than Off Track, but engagement has never actually started.
evidence: The deal sits in Pipeline, which is honest, but even that assumes a live buyer: every outreach to Derrick Staat is logged Could Not Connect (Nooks dials 2026-04-27, 2026-05-06, 2026-05-27), so no two-way conversation has ever happened.
Single Decision Maker contact role (Derrick Staat, Global Supply Chain Director) with no Economic Buyer, champion, or second thread; account C-suite exists but none are attached to this opportunity.
Close date was pushed out 60 days (2026-09-25 to 2026-11-24 on 2026-05-11 by Grace Kim) without a corresponding buyer commitment, so the forecast date reflects hope, not a buyer-driven timeline.
Product scope is a full S2P + Merlin + Agentic AI + ANA bundle at $100k, but no discovery depth, quantified pain, or business case exists to support a suite-level evaluation.
No Avoma buyer calls discovered; competition, decision process, and budget authority are all genuinely unknown, so analysis confidence is Low.

metrics.narrative: No quantified business problem, savings target, cycle-time or headcount impact is documented anywhere: the opportunity has no Avoma calls and no populated business-problem fields. The only directional signal is the Salesforce description's note that Nippon is exploring how the Autonomous Negotiation Agent could 'enhance cost savings and consistency in supplier negotiations' (source: SF description / AIS_Why, read 2026-07-06), which is an inferred interest, not a buyer-stated metric. The Next Step's plan to 'map current process to future state (light workshop style)' shows metrics discovery is intended but has not run. The move is to land the tailored demo and process-mapping session and have Derrick Staat quantify current supplier-negotiation cost, cycle time, and spend under management.

economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer is identified or engaged. The only contact role is Derrick Staat (Global Supply Chain Director, Decision Maker, primary; derrick.staat@nninc.com), a functional procurement leader who is unlikely to own budget sign-off for a full-suite S2P purchase at an enterprise insurer. The Salesforce sponsor and budget-approval fields are empty and there is no call to reveal who controls pricing. Because the buyer has never taken a live call (every dial is Could Not Connect through 2026-05-27), we have no read on the approval chain at all. The move is to reach Derrick through a channel he will answer, then ask directly who approves budget and secure an introduction to that finance or operations executive.

decision_criteria.narrative: No formal evaluation criteria, RFP requirements, or scorecard are on record; the business-requirements and priorities fields are empty and no call captured how Nippon will judge vendors. The only implied criterion is AI-led negotiation capability, inferred from the buyer's stated interest in the Autonomous Negotiation Agent 'with real scenarios and controls' (source: SF Next_Step__c / description), which signals that demonstrable, controllable AI is likely a weighted requirement. This remains inferred until confirmed on a call. The move is to use the tailored ANA demo to surface and document Nippon's must-have criteria (AI controls, integration, insurance-scale fit).

decision_process.narrative: The buyer's vendor-selection and approval process is unknown. The deal has sat in Qualified since 2026-04-28 with no stage progression and no field intel on evaluation steps or gates. The single Decision Maker contact suggests Derrick Staat owns the functional selection, but with no Economic Buyer, no champion, and no second thread, the committee and approval chain are invisible. A Zycus Source-to-Pay shared space was created and Derrick was invited (2026-04-28, to derrick.staat@outlook.com) but there is no evidence he engaged with it. The move is to have Derrick walk us through Nippon's evaluation stages and sign-off chain on the first live conversation.

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are entirely unknown this early: no MSA/SOW, legal, procurement channel, or InfoSec detail exists, and there is no Current_Contract_Expiration forcing function on record. The Next Step's intent to 'include procurement + IT stakeholder for integration confidence' shows integration and paper scoping is anticipated but not started. This is normal for a Qualified-stage deal and is not a risk yet. The move that creates visibility is adding the IT/integration stakeholder to the tailored demo so security and systems requirements surface early.

identify_pain.narrative: No specific operational or financial pain has been articulated by the buyer; the pain and compelling-event fields are empty and there are no calls. The strongest available signal is the Salesforce-described interest in using the Autonomous Negotiation Agent to improve 'cost savings and consistency in supplier negotiations' (source: SF description / AIS_Why), which points to inconsistent, manual supplier negotiation as the likely pain, but this is inferred, not confirmed by Derrick or any stakeholder. No pain owner is confirmed beyond the functional assumption that it sits with Derrick Staat's supply-chain organization. The move is to run the process-mapping workshop and have the buyer name and quantify the negotiation and contract pain in their own words.

champion.narrative: No champion exists or is developing. Derrick Staat is the sole buyer contact and a Decision Maker by role, but he has shown zero advocacy: he has not answered a single outreach (Nooks dials logged Could Not Connect on 2026-04-27, 2026-05-06, and 2026-05-27), has not engaged the shared space he was invited to on 2026-04-28, and has introduced no colleagues. There is no evidence of internal selling, agenda-driving, or executive access. Without a responsive contact the deal has no internal engine. The move ranks ahead of any commercial step: re-establish contact with Derrick through a channel he will answer, prove value with the ANA demo, and earn a first real advocacy signal (an internal intro).

competition.narrative: The competitive landscape is genuinely unknown. Competitors__c is empty, the competition synthesis fields carry nothing, and there are no calls or next-step references to any rival, incumbent, or manual baseline. It is unclear whether Nippon is running a competitive RFP, evaluating incumbents such as SAP Ariba, Coupa, or Ivalua, or comparing Zycus to a do-nothing status quo. Because the buyer has never engaged in dialogue, we have zero benchmarking intel. No competitor is asserted, per living-memory discipline (do not invent one on a zero-call run). The move is to ask Derrick during the first live conversation how Nippon handles supplier negotiation and contracting today and whether other vendors are in play, then position the Merlin/ANA agentic story as the wedge.

deal_movement.summary: The only movement since qualification is administrative and rep-side, not buyer-driven: the close date was pushed out and every attempt to reach the buyer failed to connect. No genuine two-way buyer engagement has occurred.

items.change: Stage advanced Initial Interest to Qualified (Grace Kim)
Zycus Source-to-Pay shared space created and Derrick Staat invited (no engagement recorded)
Close date pushed out 60 days, 2026-09-25 to 2026-11-24 (Grace Kim), with no buyer commitment behind it
Latest outbound call to Derrick Staat logged Could Not Connect (Nooks); last activity on the deal

competitive_position.summary: Competition is genuinely unknown, not sole-sourced: with zero buyer calls and empty competition fields, we cannot tell whether Nippon is running an RFP, weighing incumbents (SAP Ariba / Coupa / Ivalua are typical BFSI peers but NONE are named in evidence), or defaulting to manual status quo. The most likely current threat is inertia (do-nothing) given the deal has never gained buyer momentum, but that too is inferred. No competitor is named on any source, so none is listed.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: The only expressed expectation is inferred from Salesforce: Nippon wants to see the Autonomous Negotiation Agent work with 'real scenarios and controls' in an insurance-scale workflow. Fit is promising on paper (Merlin/ANA is a differentiated agentic capability) but unvalidated because no buyer conversation has tested it.

items.criterion: AI-led supplier negotiation with demonstrable controls (ANA)
items.quote: Deep dive on AI especially how negotiation agent works with real scenarios and controls
Share use cases relevant to insurance or services firms with similar scale
All outbound calls to Derrick Staat logged Could Not Connect (Nooks, through 2026-05-27)
No sponsor or budget-approver named; only a functional Decision Maker on record
No competitor named in any field, call, next step, or task
No buyer-stated or quantified pain; interest is inferred from the SF description only

best_practice_check.summary: This deal has never actually started a buyer conversation; the entire win strategy is to break through to the one contact and multi-thread from there before spending effort on a demo into silence.
flags: Break the Could-Not-Connect loop: five cold dials to Derrick Staat failed, so switch channel (a personalized ANA-scenario email or LinkedIn note) before dialing again.
Single-thread on one Decision Maker is the top risk: earn a second contact (procurement peer or IT) and a path to a budget owner as soon as a live conversation happens.
Qualify the reality behind the pushed close date: the 60-day slip to 24 Nov was rep-driven, so confirm whether Nippon has any real evaluation timeline before forecasting this deal.

items.name: Derrick Staat
items.title: Global Supply Chain Director
items.risk: Sole buyer contact and single point of failure; has never answered a live outreach (all dials Could Not Connect) and has not engaged the shared space; no advocacy and no path yet to a budget owner.

champion_strength.summary: No champion. Derrick Staat is the only buyer-side contact and, despite a Decision Maker role, has shown no engagement at all: every outreach since 2026-04-27 is logged Could Not Connect and he has not touched the shared space he was invited to on 2026-04-28. Nothing has developed and everything is missing, an intro, an agenda push, executive access, so building a first responsive relationship with Derrick is prerequisite to any deal progress.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AIS 7 (Moderate) with a specific, prospect-side pull toward the Autonomous Negotiation Agent gives a genuine AI wedge, but it is under-leveraged because the agentic story has never been put in front of the buyer live. The ANA / Merlin narrative should anchor the first real conversation.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS_Score 7 / Moderate. The Salesforce description shows a concrete ANA-specific interest (AI to improve cost savings and negotiation consistency), the strongest AI-pull signal short of a transcript, but no call exists to confirm whether it is under- or over-rated.

items.detail: No economic buyer or executive sponsor mapped; only a functional Decision Maker, and he is unengaged.
Single-threaded on one contact who does not respond; the deal has no internal advocate or second path in.
Close date pushed out 60 days on 2026-05-11 with no buyer-driven timeline behind it; forecast date is not credible.
Do-nothing / status-quo inertia: Nippon may keep negotiating suppliers manually and never move; the main threat is no-decision, not a rival.
items.source: Inference from zero engagement + empty pain fields

confidence_signals.summary: The deal is cooling toward dark: no buyer touch has ever landed and the last activity was 40 days ago. It is not yet 60 days cold, so it is recoverable, but only if a live conversation is created quickly.
items: Every outbound call logged Could Not Connect (2026-04-27, 2026-05-06, 2026-05-27).
No inbound from the buyer at any point in the deal's life.
Last activity 2026-05-27, 40 days before this sweep.

customer_preference.evidence: No two-way buyer dialogue exists; all outreach to Derrick Staat is unanswered (Could Not Connect through 2026-05-27), so there is no expressed lean toward or away from Zycus.

business_case.evidence: No ROI, savings, or automation impact quantified or articulated by the buyer; ANA cost-savings interest is inferred from the SF description only.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: This is an early Qualified full-suite deal that has never actually started: the one contact, Derrick Staat, has not answered a single outreach, so before any demo the job is to break through to him and open a second thread to a budget owner.

win_position.text: The deal has no traction because the only buyer contact, Derrick Staat (Global Supply Chain Director), has never taken a live call, with every dial logged Could Not Connect through 27 May, so there is no confirmed pain, budget owner, or competitive read to win on yet.
It is single-threaded and top-heavy: a $100k full S2P plus Merlin, Agentic AI, and ANA bundle is riding on one unresponsive functional director with no economic buyer or champion mapped.
The 24 Nov close was pushed out 60 days by the rep on 11 May with no buyer-driven timeline, so the forecast date is optimistic rather than earned.

deal_momentum.text: Momentum is effectively zero: the deal has sat in Qualified since 28 April with no stage progression and the last activity was a failed dial on 27 May, 40 days before this sweep.
No buyer has requested a meeting, named a date, or engaged the shared space they were invited to on 28 April; every touch has been rep-side and outbound.

customer_commitment.text: There is no buyer commitment of any kind, not a returned call, a requested demo, or a stated requirement; the tailored ANA demo the rep planned remains unexecuted.
The one positive is a specific, prospect-side interest in the Autonomous Negotiation Agent (AIS 7, per the Salesforce description) that gives a differentiated hook to re-open the conversation.

deal_risk.text: The dominant risk is no-decision inertia: with no engaged stakeholder and no articulated pain, Nippon can simply keep negotiating suppliers manually and let the deal lapse.
Single-thread and unmapped economic buyer mean that if Derrick stays silent the deal has no alternate path in and will go dark past 60 days.

champion.evidence: Sole contact Derrick Staat has never responded; no advocacy, no intro, shared space unengaged.

engagement.evidence: Every outreach Could Not Connect; last activity 40 days ago; zero buyer-initiated contact.

economic_buyer.evidence: No sponsor or budget owner mapped; only a functional Decision Maker.

items.expected_effect: Switching channel with a value-led hook is the most likely way to earn the first live reply and restart the deal.

items.expected_effect: Proves the differentiated agentic story and surfaces the pain and criteria needed to qualify the deal properly.

items.expected_effect: Multi-threading reduces single-point-of-failure risk and opens a path toward the budget owner.

items.action: Ask Derrick how Nippon handles supplier negotiation today and whether other vendors are in play.
items.trigger: Competitive landscape is entirely unknown; no competitor named in any source.
items.expected_effect: Reveals the incumbent or do-nothing baseline and lets us position Merlin/ANA as the wedge.

critical_signals.text: There has been no real conversation at all: every attempt to reach Derrick Staat since late April was logged as could-not-connect, most recently on 27 May.
The deal is still riding on one contact, Derrick Staat, the Global Supply Chain Director, and he has never engaged, so there is no second thread and no budget owner in view.
No rival or incumbent has surfaced; the real threat is Nippon doing nothing and continuing to negotiate suppliers manually.
A $100k full-suite bundle has no confirmed pain or budget owner behind it, so the price cannot yet be justified to anyone at Nippon.
